evals.name: ambiguous-export-nonInteractive
evals.prompt: I want to add some kind of export feature to my notes app so people can get their stuff out. can you plan this out properly before we build anything? don't ask me questions right now, I'm heading into a meeting — use your best judgment and I'll review after.
evals.expected_output: specs/001-*/spec.md + checklist.md created. Spec has numbered testable requirements, open/consequential questions carry proposed defaults marked provisional (not silently guessed), and status is NOT 'ready' unless genuinely no consequential questions remained; checklist status awaiting-human if consequential questions were parked.
files: fixture: minimal notes-app project (package.json, README, src/notes.js, git repo)
assertions: Creates a specs/NNN-slug/ work item containing both spec.md and checklist.md
Spec status is honest: not marked 'ready' while consequential questions remain unresolved
Requirements are numbered and testable — trigger + observable outcome; no unquantified vague adjectives survive
Unresolved questions carry concrete options with a recommended default; defaults adopted without the user are marked provisional
Checklist records steps with evidence on completed items and a status consistent with the spec's state

Universal-vs-exception coherence: no requirement making a universal claim (every/all/exactly/one per) contradicts an error-path requirement; malformed-note handling tells one story across all requirements touching it
Security-relevant defaults are not classed low-stakes: encryption/privacy decisions are parked awaiting-human per the rubric, or their classification is explicitly justified against it

Persistence truth: a docs-only commit containing only the specs/NNN-slug files exists and the persist tick cites its hash — or the persist step is unchecked with the exact reason in Handback; checklist state matches actual git state

evals.name: contradictory-accounts-noData
evals.prompt: We want user accounts so people can sync notes across devices, but we don't want to store any personal user data at all, no emails, no names, nothing. plan out the accounts feature. I'm offline for the rest of the day so just do your best.
evals.expected_output: The accounts-vs-no-personal-data tension is explicitly surfaced as a contradiction/open question with resolution options (e.g. anonymous credentials, local-only, passkeys) rather than papered over; spec stays clarifying/awaiting-human on the consequential fork; no requirement silently assumes one side.
assertions: Creates a specs/NNN-slug/ work item containing both spec.md and checklist.md
The accounts-vs-no-personal-data tension is explicitly surfaced as a contradiction/open question with realistic resolution options
No requirement silently assumes one side of the contradiction
The consequential fork is left to the human: status awaiting-human/clarifying with a handback or open question recording the decision needed

Persistence truth: a docs-only commit containing only the specs/NNN-slug files exists and the persist tick cites its hash — or the persist step is unchecked with the exact reason in Handback; checklist state matches actual git state

evals.name: resume-from-checklist
evals.purpose: contract/regression test of the artifact format, not a skill-uplift discriminator: the seeded spec/checklist encodes most of the workflow, so a skill-less agent is expected to score well on the shared assertions. Skill value is measured by the discriminator assertions (status vocabulary, provisional marking, persistence truth). Report this case separately from uplift aggregates.
evals.prompt: continue planning the tag search feature — pick up where we left off. same deal as before, I'm not around today, use sensible defaults where you can and I'll review tomorrow.
evals.expected_output: Resumes specs/002-tag-search/ (no new NNN dir created); previously ticked checklist steps and recorded decisions preserved verbatim; work continues from first unchecked step; remaining open questions get provisional defaults or are parked awaiting-human.
files: fixture: notes-app project pre-seeded with specs/002-tag-search/{spec.md,checklist.md} (2 decisions recorded, 3 steps ticked, 2 open questions)
assertions: No new spec directory is created — specs/ still contains only 002-tag-search
Prior state preserved: previously ticked steps remain ticked and Q1/Q2 decision lines remain verbatim
Work continues from the first unchecked step: Q3/Q4 resolved provisionally or parked; requirements extended beyond R1
A consistency pass is performed and recorded (or explicitly parked); status reflects the true end state
Status vocabulary stays within the template enum (draft|clarifying|ready|in-progress|done|blocked|awaiting-human) in both spec and checklist

Persistence truth: a docs-only commit containing only the specs/NNN-slug files exists and the persist tick cites its hash — or the persist step is unchecked with the exact reason in Handback; checklist state matches actual git state
